name: Steamed Persimmon Pudding
recipeIngredient: 3 very ripe, soft persimmons (about 1 pound), stemmed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender or food processor, puree the persimmons (with skins); set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter and sugars until light and fluffy.
3. Mix in the egg to blend thoroughly.
4. Into another bowl, sift the dry ingredients and mix into the butter mixture alternately with milk in three additions.
5. Mix in the persimmon puree and vanilla to blend thoroughly.
6. Generously butter a 1-quart mold.
7. Pour the batter into the mold and cover tightly with a buttered lid or aluminum foil.
8. Place a rack in a pot taller than the mold.
9. Place the mold on the rack and add enough boiling water to come halfway up the side of the mold.
10. Cover the pot with a lid or aluminum foil.
11. Steam the pudding over medium heat on the top of the stove or in a preheated 325-degree oven for 1 1/2 hours, checking frequently and adding boiling water to maintain the correct level.
12. Remove the mold from the pot to a cooling rack.
13. Remove the lid; set aside the mold until lukewarm.
14. Unmold onto a plate and serve warm or at room temperature.
